title: 2017 Turkish Constitutional Referendum on the transition to an executive presidential system

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on Gezici and Metropoll early 2017 trends
Estimated: Interpolated trend from Konda and ORC
Estimated: Reflects diplomatic tensions with EU

events.description: Dutch government blocks Turkish ministers from holding rallies, sparking diplomatic crisis
President Erdogan intensifies rhetoric against European 'Nazism' to mobilize nationalist base
Supreme Election Council (YSK) decides to accept unstamped ballots as valid

agents.name: Recep Tayyip Erdogan
agents.description: President of Turkey, primary proponent of the executive system

agents.name: AKP (Justice and Development Party)
agents.description: Ruling party supporting the constitutional changes

agents.name: CHP (Republican People's Party)
agents.description: Main opposition party campaigning against the changes

agents.name: MHP (Nationalist Movement Party)
agents.description: Nationalist party leadership supporting the changes

agents.name: HDP (Peoples' Democratic Party)
agents.description: Pro-Kurdish opposition party campaigning against the changes

agents.name: Conservative Anatolian Voters
agents.description: Core electoral base for the pro-referendum side

agents.name: Urban Secular Voters
agents.description: Core electoral base for the anti-referendum side

notes: Pro-side (Yes) refers to supporting the constitutional amendments to transition to an executive presidential system. Against-side (No) refers to opposing these changes. Data quality is subject to the highly polarized media environment and the contested nature of the YSK's final ballot counting procedures.
